person.name: Henry de Trafford
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
He was the successor to his grandfather. He came of age in 1336.

His name appears in several deeds during the reigns of Henry II and Richard I.

Henry made further additions to the patrimonial estates by the acquisition of lands in Sale from Thomas de Hyde, in Withington from Nigel de Longford, and in Chorlton and Beswick from the Abbot of Cockersand.

zh.Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry de Trafford於大約1292年出生在英國藍卡爾特拉福德 帕克時，他的父親Sir John de Trafford30歲，母親Agnes25歲。他於1323年與Agnes Doterinde在英國藍卡爾特拉福德 曼諾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1312年被列為member of parliament for lancashire。他去世於大約1370年在他的家鄉享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Trafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Trafford (Lancashire), Trafford in Chipping Warden (Northamptonshire), and Bridge, Mickle, and Wimbolds Trafford (Cheshire). The Northamptonshire placename probably derives from Old English træppe ‘trap’ + ford ‘ford’. The Cheshire placenames derive from Old English trōg ‘trough’ + ford. The Lancashire placename is a development of Stretford (Lancashire); most cases of the surname probably derive from this place. Early members of the de Trafford family, recorded here from the 13th century, were also recorded in medieval documents as de Stratford(e). Both the surname and the placename lost the initial S-. The manor of Trafford was carved out of Stretford township, and perhaps because its manor house was located at some distance from Stretford village, Trafford was adopted for the new settlement from an early date. See also Stratford .

variants.name: Stratford
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Stratford, for example in Bedfordshire, Buckinghamshire, Middlesex, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and Wiltshire, from Old English strǣt ‘Roman road’ (see Street ) + ford ‘ford’.

habitational name principally from Strafford Sands (now lost) in Mexborough (Yorkshire), presumed to be the ancient meeting-place of Strafforth Wapentake, and deriving from the same source as 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry de Trafford was born about 1292, in Trafford Park, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir John de Trafford, was 30 and his mother, Agnes, was 25. He married Agnes Doterinde in 1323, in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. In 1312, at the age of 21, his occupation is listed as member of parliament for lancashire. He died about 1370, in his hometown, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry de Trafford est né environ 1292, à Trafford Park, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Trafford, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agnes, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Doterinde en 1323, à Trafford Manor, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. En 1312, à l’âge de 21 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament for lancashire. Il est décédé environ 1370, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.Trafford, Lancashire, England: Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Henry de Trafford um 1292 in Trafford Park, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Trafford 30 und seine Mutter Agnes 25. Er hat 1323 in Trafford Manor, Lancashire, England Agnes Doterinde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1312, als er 21 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als member of parliament for lancashire aufgeführt. Er starb um 1370 im Alter von 80 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

ko.lifeSummary: Henry de Trafford(이)가 대략 1292년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 박에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Trafford(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1323년 영국 랭카실르 트래퍼드 먼어에서 Agnes Doterinde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1312년, 21세의 나이로 그의 직업은 member of parliament for lancashire이었다. 그는 대략 1370년 그의 고향에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry de Trafford родился около 1292 г. в местности траффорд парк, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John de Trafford было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Doterinde в 1323 г. в местности траффорд манор, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. В 1312 году, в возрасте 21 год,  он работал по профессии member of parliament for lancashire. Он умер около 1370 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 80 лет.
